1	package inputbox
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5	
6		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/help"
7		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
8		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/textarea"
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
10		"charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
11	
12		dataautocomplete "github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data/autocomplete"
13		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/autocomplete"
14		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/context"
15	)
16	
17	type Model struct {
18		ctx                *context.ProgramContext
19		textArea           textarea.Model
20		inputHelp          help.Model
21		prompt             string
22		autocomplete       *autocomplete.Model
23		autocompleteSource dataautocomplete.Source
24	}
25	
26	var inputKeys = []key.Binding{
27		key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+d"), key.WithHelp("Ctrl+d", "submit")),
28		key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+c", "esc"), key.WithHelp("Ctrl+c/esc", "cancel")),
29		autocomplete.ToggleSuggestions,
30	}
31	
32	func NewModel(ctx *context.ProgramContext) Model {
33		ta := textarea.New()
34		ta.ShowLineNumbers = true
35		ta.Prompt = ""
36		ta.CharLimit = 65536
37		base := lipgloss.NewStyle()
38		ta.SetStyles(textarea.Styles{
39			Focused: textarea.StyleState{
40				Base:       base,
41				Text:       base.Foreground(ctx.Theme.PrimaryText),
42				LineNumber: base.Foreground(ctx.Theme.FaintText),
43				CursorLine: base.Background(ctx.Theme.FaintBorder).
44					Foreground(ctx.Theme.PrimaryText),
45				CursorLineNumber: base.Foreground(ctx.Theme.SecondaryText),
46				Placeholder:      base.Foreground(ctx.Theme.FaintText),
47				EndOfBuffer:      base.Foreground(ctx.Theme.FaintText),
48			},
49		})
50		ta.Focus()
51	
52		h := help.New()
53		h.Styles = ctx.Styles.Help.BubbleStyles
54		return Model{
55			ctx:       ctx,
56			textArea:  ta,
57			inputHelp: h,
58			prompt:    "",
59		}
60	}
61	
62	func (m *Model) SetAutocomplete(ac *autocomplete.Model) {
63		m.autocomplete = ac
64	}
65	
66	func (m *Model) SetAutocompleteSource(src dataautocomplete.Source) {
67		m.autocompleteSource = src
68	}
69	
70	func (m Model) CurrentAutocompleteContext() dataautocomplete.Context {
71		if m.autocompleteSource == nil {
72			return dataautocomplete.Context{}
73		}
74	
75		return m.autocompleteSource.ExtractContext(m.textArea.Value(), m.CursorPosition())
76	}
77	
78	func (m Model) AutocompleteItemsToExclude() []string {
79		if m.autocompleteSource == nil {
80			return nil
81		}
82	
83		return m.autocompleteSource.ItemsToExclude(m.textArea.Value(), m.CursorPosition())
84	}
85	
86	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Model, tea.Cmd) {
87		var cmd tea.Cmd
88	
89		switch msg := msg.(type) {
90		case tea.KeyMsg:
91			// Allow toggling suggestions at any time
92			if m.autocomplete != nil && key.Matches(msg, autocomplete.ToggleSuggestions) {
93				if m.autocomplete.IsVisible() {
94					m.autocomplete.Suppress()
95					return m, nil
96				}
97	
98				m.autocomplete.Unsuppress()
99				currentContext := m.CurrentAutocompleteContext()
100				m.autocomplete.Show(currentContext.Content, m.AutocompleteItemsToExclude())
101				return m, nil
102			}
103	
104			// Allow navigation/selection even if the popup is hidden (as long as there are filtered results)
105			if m.autocomplete != nil &&
106				(m.autocomplete.IsVisible() || m.autocomplete.HasSuggestions()) {
107				switch {
108				case key.Matches(msg, autocomplete.PrevKey):
109					m.autocomplete.Prev()
110					return m, nil
111				case key.Matches(msg, autocomplete.NextKey):
112					m.autocomplete.Next()
113					return m, nil
114				case key.Matches(msg, autocomplete.SelectKey):
115					selected := m.autocomplete.Selected()
116					if selected != "" && m.autocompleteSource != nil {
117						currentValue := m.textArea.Value()
118						currentContext := m.CurrentAutocompleteContext()
119						newValue, newCursorPos := m.autocompleteSource.InsertSuggestion(
120							currentValue,
121							selected,
122							currentContext.Start,
123							currentContext.End,
124						)
125						m.textArea.SetValue(newValue)
126						m.textArea.SetCursorColumn(newCursorPos)
127						// Refresh autocomplete to exclude the newly-added item
128						if m.AutocompleteItemsToExclude() != nil {
129							newContext := m.CurrentAutocompleteContext()
130							m.autocomplete.Show(newContext.Content, m.AutocompleteItemsToExclude())
131						}
132					}
133					return m, nil
134				}
135			}
136		}
137	
138		m.textArea, cmd = m.textArea.Update(msg)
139		return m, cmd
140	}
141	
142	func (m Model) View() string {
143		return lipgloss.NewStyle().
144			BorderTop(true).
145			BorderStyle(lipgloss.NormalBorder()).
146			BorderForeground(m.ctx.Theme.SecondaryBorder).
147			MarginTop(1).
148			Render(
149				lipgloss.JoinVertical(
150					lipgloss.Left,
151					fmt.Sprintf("%s\n", m.prompt),
152					m.textArea.View(),
153					lipgloss.NewStyle().
154						MarginTop(1).
155						Render(m.inputHelp.ShortHelpView(inputKeys)),
156				),
157			)
158	}
159	
160	func (m Model) ViewWithAutocomplete() string {
161		autocompleteView := ""
162		if m.autocomplete != nil && m.autocomplete.IsVisible() {
163			autocompleteView = m.autocomplete.View()
164		}
165	
166		return lipgloss.NewStyle().
167			BorderTop(true).
168			BorderStyle(lipgloss.NormalBorder()).
169			BorderForeground(m.ctx.Theme.SecondaryBorder).
170			MarginTop(1).
171			Render(
172				lipgloss.JoinVertical(
173					lipgloss.Left,
174					fmt.Sprintf("%s\n", m.prompt),
175					m.textArea.View(),
176					autocompleteView,
177					lipgloss.NewStyle().
178						MarginTop(1).
179						Render(m.inputHelp.ShortHelpView(inputKeys)),
180				),
181			)
182	}
183	
184	func (m *Model) Value() string {
185		return m.textArea.Value()
186	}
187	
188	func (m *Model) SetValue(s string) {
189		m.textArea.SetValue(s)
190	}
191	
192	func (m *Model) Blur() {
193		m.textArea.Blur()
194	}
195	
196	func (m *Model) Focus() tea.Cmd {
197		return m.textArea.Focus()
198	}
199	
200	func (m *Model) SetWidth(width int) {
201		m.textArea.SetWidth(width)
202	}
203	
204	func (m *Model) SetHeight(height int) {
205		m.textArea.SetHeight(height)
206	}
207	
208	func (m *Model) SetPrompt(prompt string) {
209		m.prompt = prompt
210	}
211	
212	func (m *Model) Reset() {
213		m.textArea.Reset()
214	}
215	
216	func (m *Model) UpdateProgramContext(ctx *context.ProgramContext) {
217		m.ctx = ctx
218		m.inputHelp.Styles = ctx.Styles.Help.BubbleStyles
219	}
220	
221	// CursorPosition returns the cursor position within the current logical line
222	// in runes. This correctly handles multi-byte Unicode characters since the
223	// textarea internally uses rune-based positioning via [][]rune.
224	//
225	// Use this for single-line input contexts like comma-separated labels.
226	// For multi-line contexts (e.g., @mentions in comments), use GetAbsoluteCursorPosition.
227	func (m *Model) CursorPosition() int {
228		lineInfo := m.textArea.LineInfo()
229		return lineInfo.StartColumn + lineInfo.ColumnOffset
230	}

content.text: Now let me look at how the inputbox is used in prview and issueview, and also look at the Bubble Tea v2 textarea for clues about key handling:

1	package prview
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"image/color"
6		"regexp"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
10		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
11		"charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
12	
13		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data"
14		dataautocomplete "github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data/autocomplete"
15		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/common"
16		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/carousel"
17		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/detailedit"
18		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/prrow"
19		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/prssection"
20		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/tasks"
21		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/constants"
22		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/context"
23		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/keys"
24		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/markdown"
25		"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/utils"
26	)
27	
28	var (
29		htmlCommentRegex = regexp.MustCompile("(?U)<!--(.|[[:space:]])*-->")
30		lineCleanupRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`((\n)+|^)([^\r\n]*\|[^\r\n]*(\n)?)+`)
31		foldBodyHeight   = 8
32	)
33	
34	type Model struct {
35		ctx             *context.ProgramContext
36		sectionId       int
37		pr              *prrow.PullRequest
38		width           int
39		carousel        carousel.Model
40		editor          detailedit.Controller
41		summaryViewMore bool
42	}
43	
44	var tabs = []string{" Overview", " Activity", " Commits", " Checks", " Files Changed"}
45	
46	func NewModel(ctx *context.ProgramContext) Model {
47		c := carousel.New(
48			carousel.WithItems(tabs),
49			carousel.WithWidth(ctx.MainContentWidth),
50		)
51	
52		return Model{
53			pr:       nil,
54			carousel: c,
55			editor:   detailedit.New(ctx),
56		}
57	}
58	
59	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Model, tea.Cmd) {
60		editor, cmd, submit, handled := m.editor.Update(msg)
61		m.editor = editor
62	
63		if submit != nil {
64			if m.pr == nil {
65				return m, nil
66			}
67	
68			sid := tasks.SectionIdentifier{Id: m.sectionId, Type: prssection.SectionType}
69	
70			switch submit.Mode {
71			case detailedit.ModeComment:
72				if len(strings.TrimSpace(submit.Value)) != 0 {
73					return m, tasks.CommentOnPR(m.ctx, sid, m.pr.Data.Primary, submit.Value)
74				}
75				return m, nil
76	
77			case detailedit.ModeApprove:
78				comment := ""
79				if len(strings.TrimSpace(submit.Value)) != 0 {
80					comment = submit.Value
81				}
82				return m, tasks.ApprovePR(m.ctx, sid, m.pr.Data.Primary, comment)
83	
84			case detailedit.ModeAssign:
85				usernames := dataautocomplete.AllWords(submit.Value)
86				if len(usernames) > 0 {
87					return m, tasks.AssignPR(m.ctx, sid, m.pr.Data.Primary, usernames)
88				}
89				return m, nil
90	
91			case detailedit.ModeUnassign:
92				usernames := dataautocomplete.AllWords(submit.Value)
93				if len(usernames) > 0 {
94					return m, tasks.UnassignPR(m.ctx, sid, m.pr.Data.Primary, usernames)
95				}
96				return m, nil
97	
98			case detailedit.ModeLabel:
99				labels := dataautocomplete.CurrentLabels(submit.Value)
100				if len(labels) > 0 || len(m.pr.Data.Primary.Labels.Nodes) > 0 {
101					return m, m.label(labels)
102				}
103				return m, nil
104			}
105		}
106		if handled {
107			return m, cmd
108		}
109	
110		if keyMsg, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
111			switch {
112			case key.Matches(keyMsg, keys.PRKeys.PrevSidebarTab):
113				m.carousel.MoveLeft()
114			case key.Matches(keyMsg, keys.PRKeys.NextSidebarTab):
115				m.carousel.MoveRight()
116			}
117		}
118	
119		return m, cmd
120	}
121	
122	func (m Model) View() string {
123		header := strings.Builder{}
124	
125		header.WriteString(m.renderFullNameAndNumber())
126		header.WriteString("\n")
127	
128		header.WriteString(m.renderTitle())
129		header.WriteString("\n\n")
130		header.WriteString(m.renderBranches())
131		header.WriteString("\n\n")
132		header.WriteString(m.renderAuthor())
133		header.WriteString("\n\n")
134		header.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(m.width).
135			Border(lipgloss.NormalBorder(), false, false, true, false).
136			BorderForeground(m.ctx.Theme.FaintBorder).
137			Render(m.carousel.View()),
138		)
139	
140		header.WriteString("\n")
141	
142		body := strings.Builder{}
143	
144		switch m.carousel.SelectedItem() {
145		case tabs[0]:
146			reviewers := m.renderRequestedReviewers()
147			if reviewers != "" {
148				body.WriteString(reviewers)
149				body.WriteString("\n\n")
150			}
151	
152			labels := m.renderLabels()
153			if labels != "" {
154				body.WriteString(labels)
155				body.WriteString("\n\n")
156			}
157	
158			body.WriteString(m.renderSummary())
159			body.WriteString("\n\n")
160			body.WriteString(
161				m.ctx.Styles.Common.MainTextStyle.MarginBottom(1).Underline(true).Render(" Changes"),
162			)
163			body.WriteString("\n")
164			body.WriteString(m.renderChangesOverview())
165			body.WriteString("\n\n")
166			body.WriteString(
167				m.ctx.Styles.Common.MainTextStyle.MarginBottom(1).Underline(true).Render(" Checks"),
168			)
169			body.WriteString("\n")
170			body.WriteString(m.renderChecksOverview())
171	
172			if editorView := m.editor.View(); editorView != "" {
173				body.WriteString(editorView)
174			}
175	
176		case tabs[1]:
177			body.WriteString(m.renderActivity())
178		case tabs[2]:
179			body.WriteString(m.renderCommits())
180		case tabs[3]:
181			body.WriteString(m.renderChecksOverview())
182			body.WriteString("\n\n")
183			body.WriteString(m.renderChecks())
184		case tabs[4]:
185			body.WriteString(m.renderChangedFiles())
186		}
187	
188		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,
189			header.String(),
190			lipgloss.NewStyle().Padding(0, m.ctx.Styles.Sidebar.ContentPadding).Render(body.String()),
191		)
192	}
193	
194	func (m *Model) renderFullNameAndNumber() string {
195		return common.RenderPreviewHeader(
196			m.ctx.Theme,
197			m.width,
198			fmt.Sprintf(
199				"%s · #%d",
200				m.pr.Data.Primary.GetRepoNameWithOwner(),
201				m.pr.Data.Primary.GetNumber(),
202			),
203		)
204	}
205	
206	func (m *Model) renderTitle() string {
207		return common.RenderPreviewTitle(
208			m.ctx.Theme,
209			m.ctx.Styles.Common,
210			m.width,
211			m.pr.Data.Primary.Title,
212		)
213	}
214	
215	func (m *Model) renderBranches() string {
216		return lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Left,
217			" ",
218			m.renderStatusPill(),
219			" ",
220			lipgloss.NewStyle().
221				Foreground(m.ctx.Theme.SecondaryText).
222				Render(m.pr.Data.Primary.BaseRefName+"  "+m.pr.Data.Primary.HeadRefName))
223	}
224	
225	func (m *Model) renderStatusPill() string {
226		var bgColor color.Color
227		switch m.pr.Data.Primary.State {
228		case "OPEN":
229			if m.pr.Data.Primary.IsDraft {
230				bgColor = m.ctx.Theme.FaintText.Dark
231			} else {
232				bgColor = m.ctx.Styles.Colors.OpenPR.Dark
233			}
234		case "CLOSED":
235			bgColor = m.ctx.Styles.Colors.ClosedPR.Dark
236		case "MERGED":
237			bgColor = m.ctx.Styles.Colors.MergedPR.Dark
238		}
239	
240		return m.ctx.Styles.PrView.PillStyle.
241			BorderForeground(bgColor).
242			Background(bgColor).
243			Render(m.pr.RenderState())
244	}
245	
246	func (m *Model) renderLabels() string {
247		width := m.getIndentedContentWidth()
248		labels := m.pr.Data.Primary.Labels.Nodes
249		style := m.ctx.Styles.PrView.PillStyle
250		if len(labels) == 0 {
251			return ""
252		}
253	
254		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(
255			lipgloss.Left,
256			m.ctx.Styles.Common.MainTextStyle.Underline(true).Bold(true).Render(
257				fmt.Sprintf("%s Labels", constants.LabelsIcon)),
258			"",
259			common.RenderLabels(labels, common.LabelOpts{
260				Width:     width,
261				PillStyle: style,
262			}),
263		)
264	}
265	
266	type reviewerItem struct {
267		text string
268	}
269	
270	func (m *Model) renderRequestedReviewers() string {
271		if !m.pr.Data.IsEnriched {
272			return lipgloss.JoinVertical(
273				lipgloss.Left,
274				m.ctx.Styles.Common.MainTextStyle.Underline(true).Bold(true).Render(
275					fmt.Sprintf("%s Reviewers", constants.CodeReviewIcon)),
276				"",
277				lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(
278					lipgloss.Top,
279					m.ctx.Styles.Common.WaitingGlyph,
280					" ",
281					m.ctx.Styles.Common.FaintTextStyle.Render("Loading..."),
282				),
283			)
284		}
285	
286		reviewRequests := m.pr.Data.Enriched.ReviewRequests.Nodes
287		reviews := m.pr.Data.Enriched.Reviews.Nodes
288		suggestedReviewers := m.pr.Data.Enriched.SuggestedReviewers
289	
290		if len(reviewRequests) == 0 && len(reviews) == 0 && len(suggestedReviewers) == 0 {
291			return ""
292		}
293	
294		reviewStates := make(map[string]string)
295		for _, review := range reviews {
296			login := review.Author.Login
297			existingState := reviewStates[login]
298			// Don't override APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED with COMMENTED
299			if review.State == "COMMENTED" &&
300				(existingState == "APPROVED" || existingState == "CHANGES_REQUESTED") {
301				continue
302			}
303			reviewStates[login] = review.State
304		}
305	
306		reviewerItems := make([]reviewerItem, 0)
307		faintStyle := m.ctx.Styles.Common.FaintTextStyle
308		reviewerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(m.ctx.Theme.FaintText)
309		successStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(m.ctx.Theme.SuccessText)
310		errorStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(m.ctx.Theme.ErrorText)
311	
312		shownReviewers := make(map[string]bool)
313	
314		for _, req := range reviewRequests {
315			displayName := req.GetReviewerDisplayName()
316			if displayName == "" {
317				continue
318			}
319			shownReviewers[displayName] = true
320	
321			var reviewerStr string
322			stateIcon := ""
323			if state, hasReview := reviewStates[displayName]; hasReview && state == "COMMENTED" {
324				stateIcon = m.ctx.Styles.Common.CommentGlyph
325			} else {
326				stateIcon = m.ctx.Styles.Common.WaitingDotGlyph
327			}
328	
329			if req.IsTeam() {
330				reviewerStr += reviewerStyle.Render(displayName)
331			} else {
332				reviewerStr += reviewerStyle.Render("@" + displayName)
333			}
334	
335			if req.AsCodeOwner {
336				reviewerStr = lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top,
337					faintStyle.Render(constants.OwnerIcon), " ", reviewerStr)
338			}
339			reviewerStr = lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, stateIcon, " ", reviewerStr)
340	
341			reviewerItems = append(reviewerItems, reviewerItem{text: reviewerStr})
342		}
343	
344		for login, state := range reviewStates {
345			if shownReviewers[login] {
346				continue
347			}
348			if state != "APPROVED" && state != "CHANGES_REQUESTED" && state != "COMMENTED" {
349				continue
350			}
351			shownReviewers[login] = true
352	
353			var stateIcon string
354			switch state {
355			case "APPROVED":
356				stateIcon = successStyle.Render(constants.ApprovedIcon)
357			case "CHANGES_REQUESTED":
358				stateIcon = errorStyle.Render(constants.ChangesRequestedIcon)
359			case "COMMENTED":
360				stateIcon = m.ctx.Styles.Common.CommentGlyph
361			}
362			reviewerStr := stateIcon + " " + reviewerStyle.Render("@"+login)
363	
364			reviewerItems = append(reviewerItems, reviewerItem{text: reviewerStr})
365		}
366	
367		// Show suggested reviewers (= code owners) who haven't been requested or reviewed yet
368		for _, suggested := range suggestedReviewers {
369			login := suggested.Reviewer.Login
370			if shownReviewers[login] {
371				continue
372			}
373			if suggested.IsAuthor {
374				continue
375			}
376			shownReviewers[login] = true
377	
378			reviewerStr := lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top,
379				faintStyle.Render(constants.OwnerIcon), " ",
380				faintStyle.Render("@"+login),
381			)
382	
383			reviewerItems = append(reviewerItems, reviewerItem{text: reviewerStr})
384		}
385	
386		if len(reviewerItems) == 0 {
387			return ""
388		}
389	
390		width := m.getIndentedContentWidth()
391		var rows []string
392		var currentRow strings.Builder
393		currentRowWidth := 0
394	
395		for i, item := range reviewerItems {
396			itemWidth := lipgloss.Width(item.text)
397			separator := ", "
398			separatorWidth := lipgloss.Width(separator)
399	
400			// Check if adding this item would exceed the width
401			needsSeparator := i < len(reviewerItems)-1
402			totalItemWidth := itemWidth
403			if needsSeparator {
404				totalItemWidth += separatorWidth
405			}
406	
407			if currentRowWidth > 0 && currentRowWidth+totalItemWidth > width {
408				// Start a new row
409				rows = append(rows, currentRow.String())
410				currentRow.Reset()
411				currentRowWidth = 0
412			}
413	
414			currentRow.WriteString(item.text)
415			currentRowWidth += itemWidth
416	
417			if needsSeparator {
418				currentRow.WriteString(separator)
419				currentRowWidth += separatorWidth
420			}
421		}
422	
423		// Add the last row
424		if currentRow.Len() > 0 {
425			rows = append(rows, currentRow.String())
426		}
427	
428		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(
429			lipgloss.Left,
430			m.ctx.Styles.Common.MainTextStyle.Underline(true).Bold(true).Render(
431				fmt.Sprintf("%s Reviewers", constants.CodeReviewIcon)),
432			"",
433			strings.Join(rows, "\n"),
434		)
435	}

457	func (m *Model) renderSummary() string {
458		width := m.getIndentedContentWidth()
459		// Strip HTML comments from body and cleanup body.
460		body := htmlCommentRegex.ReplaceAllString(m.pr.Data.Primary.Body, "")
461		body = lineCleanupRegex.ReplaceAllString(body, "")
462	
463		desc := m.ctx.Styles.Common.MainTextStyle.Bold(true).Underline(true).Render(" Summary")
464		title := lipgloss.JoinVertical(
465			lipgloss.Left,
466			desc,
467			"",
468		)
469		sbody := lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(m.getIndentedContentWidth())
470		body = strings.TrimSpace(body)
471		if body == "" {
472			return lipgloss.JoinVertical(
473				lipgloss.Left,
474				title,
475				sbody.Italic(true).Foreground(m.ctx.Theme.FaintText).Render("No description provided."),
476			)
477		}
478	
479		markdownRenderer := markdown.GetMarkdownRenderer(width)
480		rendered, err := markdownRenderer.Render(body)
481		if err != nil {
482			return ""
483		}
484	
485		bodyHeight := lipgloss.Height(rendered)
486		if !m.summaryViewMore && bodyHeight > foldBodyHeight {
487			rendered = lipgloss.NewStyle().MaxHeight(foldBodyHeight).Render(rendered)
488			rendered = lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,
489				rendered,
490				"",
491				lipgloss.PlaceHorizontal(m.getIndentedContentWidth(), lipgloss.Center,
492					lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(
493						lipgloss.Top,
494						lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Italic(true).Render("Press "),
495						lipgloss.NewStyle().
496							Background(m.ctx.Theme.SelectedBackground).
497							Foreground(m.ctx.Theme.PrimaryText).
498							Render("e"),
499						lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Italic(true).Render(" to read more..."),
500					),
501				),
502			)
503		}
504	
505		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, title,
506			lipgloss.NewStyle().
507				Width(width).
508				MaxWidth(width).
509				Align(lipgloss.Left).
510				Render(rendered),
511		)
512	}

479	// insertRunesFromUserInput inserts runes at the current cursor position.
480	func (m *Model) insertRunesFromUserInput(runes []rune) {
481		// Clean up any special characters in the input provided by the
482		// clipboard. This avoids bugs due to e.g. tab characters and
483		// whatnot.
484		runes = m.san().Sanitize(runes)
485	
486		if m.CharLimit > 0 {
487			availSpace := m.CharLimit - m.Length()
488			// If the char limit's been reached, cancel.
489			if availSpace <= 0 {
490				return
491			}
492			// If there's not enough space to paste the whole thing cut the pasted
493			// runes down so they'll fit.
494			if availSpace < len(runes) {
495				runes = runes[:availSpace]
496			}
497		}
498	
499		// Split the input into lines.
500		var lines [][]rune
501		lstart := 0
502		for i := range runes {
503			if runes[i] == '\n' {
504				// Queue a line to become a new row in the text area below.
505				// Beware to clamp the max capacity of the slice, to ensure no
506				// data from different rows get overwritten when later edits
507				// will modify this line.
508				lines = append(lines, runes[lstart:i:i])
509				lstart = i + 1
510			}
511		}
512		if lstart <= len(runes) {
513			// The last line did not end with a newline character.
514			// Take it now.
515			lines = append(lines, runes[lstart:])
516		}
517	
518		// Obey the maximum line limit.
519		if maxLines > 0 && len(m.value)+len(lines)-1 > maxLines {
520			allowedHeight := max(0, maxLines-len(m.value)+1)
521			lines = lines[:allowedHeight]
522		}
523	
524		if len(lines) == 0 {
525			// Nothing left to insert.
526			return
527		}
528	
529		// Save the remainder of the original line at the current
530		// cursor position.
531		tail := make([]rune, len(m.value[m.row][m.col:]))
532		copy(tail, m.value[m.row][m.col:])
533	
534		// Paste the first line at the current cursor position.
535		m.value[m.row] = append(m.value[m.row][:m.col], lines[0]...)
536		m.col += len(lines[0])
537	
538		if numExtraLines := len(lines) - 1; numExtraLines > 0 {
539			// Add the new lines.
540			// We try to reuse the slice if there's already space.
541			var newGrid [][]rune
542			if cap(m.value) >= len(m.value)+numExtraLines {
543				// Can reuse the extra space.
544				newGrid = m.value[:len(m.value)+numExtraLines]
545			} else {
546				// No space left; need a new slice.
547				newGrid = make([][]rune, len(m.value)+numExtraLines)
548				copy(newGrid, m.value[:m.row+1])
549			}
550			// Add all the rows that were after the cursor in the original
551			// grid at the end of the new grid.
552			copy(newGrid[m.row+1+numExtraLines:], m.value[m.row+1:])
553			m.value = newGrid
554			// Insert all the new lines in the middle.
555			for _, l := range lines[1:] {
556				m.row++
557				m.value[m.row] = l
558				m.col = len(l)
559			}
560		}
561	
562		// Finally add the tail at the end of the last line inserted.
563		m.value[m.row] = append(m.value[m.row], tail...)
564	
565		m.SetCursorColumn(m.col)
566	}

620	// setCursorLineRelative moves the cursor by the given number of lines. Negative
621	// values move the cursor up, positive values move the cursor down.
622	func (m *Model) setCursorLineRelative(delta int) {
623		if delta == 0 {
624			return
625		}
626	
627		li := m.LineInfo()
628		charOffset := max(m.lastCharOffset, li.CharOffset)
629		m.lastCharOffset = charOffset
630	
631		// 2 columns to account for the trailing space wrapping.
632		const trailingSpace = 2
633	
634		if delta > 0 { //nolint:nestif
635			// Moving down.
636			for range delta {
637				if li.RowOffset+1 >= li.Height && m.row < len(m.value)-1 {
638					m.row++
639					m.col = 0
640				} else {
641					// Move the cursor to the start of the next virtual line.
642					m.col = min(li.StartColumn+li.Width+trailingSpace, len(m.value[m.row])-1)
643				}
644				li = m.LineInfo()
645			}
646		} else {
647			// Moving up.
648			for range -delta {
649				if li.RowOffset <= 0 && m.row > 0 {
650					m.row--
651					m.col = len(m.value[m.row])
652				} else {
653					// Move the cursor to the end of the previous line.
654					m.col = li.StartColumn - trailingSpace
655				}
656				li = m.LineInfo()
657			}
658		}
659	
660		nli := m.LineInfo()
661		m.col = nli.StartColumn
662	
663		if nli.Width <= 0 {
664			m.repositionView()
665			return
666		}
667	
668		offset := 0
669		for offset < charOffset {
670			if m.row >= len(m.value) || m.col >= len(m.value[m.row]) || offset >= nli.CharWidth-1 {
671				break
672			}
673			offset += rw.RuneWidth(m.value[m.row][m.col])
674			m.col++
675		}
676		m.repositionView()
677	}

745	// Word returns the word at the cursor position.
746	// A word is delimited by spaces or line-breaks.
747	func (m *Model) Word() string {
748		line := m.value[m.row]
749		col := m.col - 1
750	
751		if col < 0 {
752			return ""
753		}
754	
755		// If cursor is beyond the line, return empty string
756		if col >= len(line) {
757			return ""
758		}
759	
760		// If cursor is on a space, return empty string
761		if unicode.IsSpace(line[col]) {
762			return ""
763		}
764	
765		// Find the start of the word by moving left
766		start := col
767		for start > 0 && !unicode.IsSpace(line[start-1]) {
768			start--
769		}
770	
771		// Find the end of the word by moving right
772		end := col
773		for end < len(line) && !unicode.IsSpace(line[end]) {
774			end++
775		}
776	
777		return string(line[start:end])
778	}

798	// the cursor blink should be reset. If input is masked delete everything after
799	// the cursor so as not to reveal word breaks in the masked input.

804	
805	// transposeLeft exchanges the runes at the cursor and immediately
806	// before. No-op if the cursor is at the beginning of the line.  If
807	// the cursor is not at the end of the line yet, moves the cursor to
808	// the right.
809	func (m *Model) transposeLeft() {
810		if m.col == 0 || len(m.value[m.row]) < 2 {
811			return
812		}
813		if m.col >= len(m.value[m.row]) {
814			m.SetCursorColumn(m.col - 1)
815		}
816		m.value[m.row][m.col-1], m.value[m.row][m.col] = m.value[m.row][m.col], m.value[m.row][m.col-1]
817		if m.col < len(m.value[m.row]) {
818			m.SetCursorColumn(m.col + 1)
819		}
820	}
821	
822	// deleteWordLeft deletes the word left to the cursor. Returns whether or not
823	// the cursor blink should be reset.
824	func (m *Model) deleteWordLeft() {
825		if m.col == 0 || len(m.value[m.row]) == 0 {
826			return
827		}
828	
829		// Linter note: it's critical that we acquire the initial cursor position
830		// here prior to altering it via SetCursor() below. As such, moving this
831		// call into the corresponding if clause does not apply here.
832		oldCol := m.col
833	
834		m.SetCursorColumn(m.col - 1)
835		for unicode.IsSpace(m.value[m.row][m.col]) {
836			if m.col <= 0 {
837				break
838			}
839			// ignore series of whitespace before cursor
840			m.SetCursorColumn(m.col - 1)
841		}
842	
843		for m.col > 0 {
844			if !unicode.IsSpace(m.value[m.row][m.col]) {
845				m.SetCursorColumn(m.col - 1)
846			} else {
847				if m.col > 0 {
848					// keep the previous space
849					m.SetCursorColumn(m.col + 1)
850				}
851				break
852			}
853		}
854	
855		if oldCol > len(m.value[m.row]) {
856			m.value[m.row] = m.value[m.row][:m.col]
857		} else {
858			m.value[m.row] = append(m.value[m.row][:m.col], m.value[m.row][oldCol:]...)
859		}
860	}
861	
862	// deleteWordRight deletes the word right to the cursor.
863	func (m *Model) deleteWordRight() {
864		if m.col >= len(m.value[m.row]) || len(m.value[m.row]) == 0 {
865			return
866		}
867	
868		oldCol := m.col
869	
870		for m.col < len(m.value[m.row]) && unicode.IsSpace(m.value[m.row][m.col]) {
871			// ignore series of whitespace after cursor
872			m.SetCursorColumn(m.col + 1)
873		}
874	
875		for m.col < len(m.value[m.row]) {
876			if !unicode.IsSpace(m.value[m.row][m.col]) {
877				m.SetCursorColumn(m.col + 1)
878			} else {
879				break
880			}
881		}
882	
883		if m.col > len(m.value[m.row]) {
884			m.value[m.row] = m.value[m.row][:oldCol]
885		} else {
886			m.value[m.row] = append(m.value[m.row][:oldCol], m.value[m.row][m.col:]...)
887		}
888	
889		m.SetCursorColumn(oldCol)
890	}

893	func (m *Model) characterRight() {
894		if m.col < len(m.value[m.row]) {
895			m.SetCursorColumn(m.col + 1)
896		} else {
897			if m.row < len(m.value)-1 {
898				m.row++
899				m.CursorStart()
900			}
901		}
902	}

904	// characterLeft moves the cursor one character to the left.
905	// If insideLine is set, the cursor is moved to the last
906	// character in the previous line, instead of one past that.
907	func (m *Model) characterLeft(insideLine bool) {
908		if m.col == 0 && m.row != 0 {
909			m.row--
910			m.CursorEnd()
911			if !insideLine {
912				return
913			}
914		}
915		if m.col > 0 {
916			m.SetCursorColumn(m.col - 1)
917		}
918	}

920	// wordLeft moves the cursor one word to the left. Returns whether or not the
921	// cursor blink should be reset. If input is masked, move input to the start
922	// so as not to reveal word breaks in the masked input.
923	func (m *Model) wordLeft() {
924		for {
925			m.characterLeft(true /* insideLine */)
926			if m.col < len(m.value[m.row]) && !unicode.IsSpace(m.value[m.row][m.col]) {
927				break
928			}
929		}
930	
931		for m.col > 0 {
932			if unicode.IsSpace(m.value[m.row][m.col-1]) {
933				break
934			}
935			m.SetCursorColumn(m.col - 1)
936		}
937	}

939	// wordRight moves the cursor one word to the right. Returns whether or not the
940	// cursor blink should be reset. If the input is masked, move input to the end
941	// so as not to reveal word breaks in the masked input.
942	func (m *Model) wordRight() {
943		m.doWordRight(func(int, int) { /* nothing */ })
944	}
945	
946	func (m *Model) doWordRight(fn func(charIdx int, pos int)) {
947		// Skip spaces forward.
948		for m.col >= len(m.value[m.row]) || unicode.IsSpace(m.value[m.row][m.col]) {
949			if m.row == len(m.value)-1 && m.col == len(m.value[m.row]) {
950				// End of text.
951				break
952			}
953			m.characterRight()
954		}
955	
956		charIdx := 0
957		for m.col < len(m.value[m.row]) {
958			if unicode.IsSpace(m.value[m.row][m.col]) {
959				break
960			}
961			fn(charIdx, m.col)
962			m.SetCursorColumn(m.col + 1)
963			charIdx++
964		}
965	}

990	// LineInfo returns the number of characters from the start of the
991	// (soft-wrapped) line and the (soft-wrapped) line width.
992	func (m Model) LineInfo() LineInfo {
993		grid := m.memoizedWrap(m.value[m.row], m.width)
994	
995		// Find out which line we are currently on. This can be determined by the
996		// m.col and counting the number of runes that we need to skip.
997		var counter int
998		for i, line := range grid {
999			// We've found the line that we are on
1000			if counter+len(line) == m.col && i+1 < len(grid) {
1001				// We wrap around to the next line if we are at the end of the
1002				// previous line so that we can be at the very beginning of the row
1003				return LineInfo{
1004					CharOffset:   0,
1005					ColumnOffset: 0,
1006					Height:       len(grid),
1007					RowOffset:    i + 1,
1008					StartColumn:  m.col,
1009					Width:        len(grid[i+1]),
1010					CharWidth:    uniseg.StringWidth(string(line)),
1011				}
1012			}
1013	
1014			if counter+len(line) >= m.col {
1015				return LineInfo{
1016					CharOffset:   uniseg.StringWidth(string(line[:max(0, m.col-counter)])),
1017					ColumnOffset: m.col - counter,
1018					Height:       len(grid),
1019					RowOffset:    i,
1020					StartColumn:  counter,
1021					Width:        len(line),
1022					CharWidth:    uniseg.StringWidth(string(line)),
1023				}
1024			}
1025	
1026			counter += len(line)
1027		}
1028		return LineInfo{}
1029	}

1031	// repositionView repositions the view of the viewport based on the defined
1032	// scrolling behavior.
1033	func (m *Model) repositionView() {
1034		minimum := m.viewport.YOffset()
1035		maximum := minimum + m.viewport.Height() - 1
1036		if row := m.cursorLineNumber(); row < minimum {
1037			m.viewport.ScrollUp(minimum - row)
1038		} else if row > maximum {
1039			m.viewport.ScrollDown(row - maximum)
1040		}
1041	}

1051		m.SetCursorColumn(0)
1052		m.repositionView()

1056	func (m *Model) MoveToEnd() {
1057		m.row = len(m.value) - 1
1058		m.SetCursorColumn(len(m.value[m.row]))
1059		m.repositionView()

1062	// PageUp moves the cursor up by one page. First call snaps to the first visible
1063	// line, subsequent calls move up by a full page.
1064	func (m *Model) PageUp() {
1065		// If not on the first visible line, snap to it.
1066		if offset := m.viewport.YOffset() - m.cursorLineNumber(); offset < 0 {
1067			m.setCursorLineRelative(offset)
1068			return
1069		}
1070	
1071		// Already on first visible line, move up by a full page.
1072		m.setCursorLineRelative(-m.height)
1073	}
1074	
1075	// PageDown moves the cursor down by one page. First call snaps to the last
1076	// visible line, subsequent calls move down by a full page.
1077	func (m *Model) PageDown() {
1078		// If not on the last visible line, snap to it.
1079		if offset := m.cursorLineNumber() - m.viewport.YOffset(); offset < m.height-1 {
1080			m.setCursorLineRelative(m.height - 1 - offset)
1081			return
1082		}
1083	
1084		// Already on last visible line, move down by a full page.
1085		m.setCursorLineRelative(m.height)
1086	}

1088	// SetWidth sets the width of the textarea to fit exactly within the given width.
1089	// This means that the textarea will account for the width of the prompt and
1090	// whether or not line numbers are being shown.
1091	//
1092	// Ensure that SetWidth is called after setting the Prompt and ShowLineNumbers,
1093	// It is important that the width of the textarea be exactly the given width
1094	// and no more.
1095	func (m *Model) SetWidth(w int) {
1096		// Update prompt width only if there is no prompt function as
1097		// [SetPromptFunc] updates the prompt width when it is called.
1098		if m.promptFunc == nil {
1099			// XXX: Do we even need this or can we calculate the prompt width
1100			// at render time?
1101			m.promptWidth = uniseg.StringWidth(m.Prompt)
1102		}
1103	
1104		// Add base style borders and padding to reserved outer width.
1105		reservedOuter := m.activeStyle().Base.GetHorizontalFrameSize()
1106	
1107		// Add prompt width to reserved inner width.
1108		reservedInner := m.promptWidth
1109	
1110		// Add line number width to reserved inner width.
1111		if m.ShowLineNumbers {
1112			// XXX: this was originally documented as needing "1 cell" but was,
1113			// in practice, effectively hardcoded to 2 cells. We can, and should,
1114			// reduce this to one gap and update the tests accordingly.
1115			const gap = 2
1116	
1117			// Number of digits plus 1 cell for the margin.
1118			reservedInner += numDigits(m.MaxHeight) + gap
1119		}
1120	
1121		// Input width must be at least one more than the reserved inner and outer
1122		// width. This gives us a minimum input width of 1.
1123		minWidth := reservedInner + reservedOuter + 1
1124		inputWidth := max(w, minWidth)
1125	
1126		// Input width must be no more than maximum width.
1127		if m.MaxWidth > 0 {
1128			inputWidth = min(inputWidth, m.MaxWidth)
1129		}
1130	
1131		// Since the width of the viewport and input area is dependent on the width of
1132		// borders, prompt and line numbers, we need to calculate it by subtracting
1133		// the reserved width from them.
1134	
1135		m.viewport.SetWidth(inputWidth - reservedOuter)
1136		m.width = inputWidth - reservedOuter - reservedInner
1137	}
1138	
1139	// SetPromptFunc supersedes the Prompt field and sets a dynamic prompt instead.
1140	//
1141	// If the function returns a prompt that is shorter than the specified
1142	// promptWidth, it will be padded to the left. If it returns a prompt that is
1143	// longer, display artifacts may occur; the caller is responsible for computing
1144	// an adequate promptWidth.
1145	func (m *Model) SetPromptFunc(promptWidth int, fn func(PromptInfo) string) {
1146		m.promptFunc = fn
1147		m.promptWidth = promptWidth
1148	}

1155	// SetHeight sets the height of the textarea.
1156	func (m *Model) SetHeight(h int) {
1157		if m.MaxHeight > 0 {
1158			m.height = clamp(h, minHeight, m.MaxHeight)
1159			m.viewport.SetHeight(clamp(h, minHeight, m.MaxHeight))
1160		} else {
1161			m.height = max(h, minHeight)
1162			m.viewport.SetHeight(max(h, minHeight))
1163		}
1164	
1165		m.repositionView()
1166	}

1168	// Update is the Bubble Tea update loop.
1169	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Model, tea.Cmd) {
1170		if !m.focus {
1171			m.virtualCursor.Blur()
1172			return m, nil
1173		}
1174	
1175		// Used to determine if the cursor should blink.
1176		oldRow, oldCol := m.cursorLineNumber(), m.col
1177	
1178		var cmds []tea.Cmd
1179	
1180		if m.value[m.row] == nil {
1181			m.value[m.row] = make([]rune, 0)
1182		}
1183	
1184		if m.MaxHeight > 0 && m.MaxHeight != m.cache.Capacity() {
1185			m.cache = memoization.NewMemoCache[line, [][]rune](m.MaxHeight)
1186		}
1187	
1188		switch msg := msg.(type) {
1189		case tea.PasteMsg:
1190			m.insertRunesFromUserInput([]rune(msg.Content))
1191		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
1192			switch {
1193			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.DeleteAfterCursor):
1194				m.col = clamp(m.col, 0, len(m.value[m.row]))
1195				if m.col >= len(m.value[m.row]) {
1196					m.mergeLineBelow(m.row)
1197					break
1198				}
1199				m.deleteAfterCursor()
1200			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.DeleteBeforeCursor):
1201				m.col = clamp(m.col, 0, len(m.value[m.row]))
1202				if m.col <= 0 {
1203					m.mergeLineAbove(m.row)
1204					break
1205				}
1206				m.deleteBeforeCursor()
1207			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.DeleteCharacterBackward):
1208				m.col = clamp(m.col, 0, len(m.value[m.row]))
1209				if m.col <= 0 {
1210					m.mergeLineAbove(m.row)
1211					break
1212				}
1213				if len(m.value[m.row]) > 0 {
1214					m.value[m.row] = append(m.value[m.row][:max(0, m.col-1)], m.value[m.row][m.col:]...)
1215					if m.col > 0 {
1216						m.SetCursorColumn(m.col - 1)
1217					}
1218				}
1219			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.DeleteCharacterForward):
1220				if len(m.value[m.row]) > 0 && m.col < len(m.value[m.row]) {
1221					m.value[m.row] = slices.Delete(m.value[m.row], m.col, m.col+1)
1222				}
1223				if m.col >= len(m.value[m.row]) {
1224					m.mergeLineBelow(m.row)
1225					break
1226				}
1227			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.DeleteWordBackward):
1228				if m.col <= 0 {
1229					m.mergeLineAbove(m.row)
1230					break
1231				}
1232				m.deleteWordLeft()
1233			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.DeleteWordForward):
1234				m.col = clamp(m.col, 0, len(m.value[m.row]))
1235				if m.col >= len(m.value[m.row]) {
1236					m.mergeLineBelow(m.row)
1237					break
1238				}
1239				m.deleteWordRight()
1240			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.InsertNewline):
1241				if m.MaxHeight > 0 && len(m.value) >= m.MaxHeight {
1242					return m, nil
1243				}
1244				m.col = clamp(m.col, 0, len(m.value[m.row]))
1245				m.splitLine(m.row, m.col)
1246			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.LineEnd):
1247				m.CursorEnd()
1248			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.LineStart):
1249				m.CursorStart()
1250			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.CharacterForward):
1251				m.characterRight()
1252			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.LineNext):
1253				m.CursorDown()
1254			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.WordForward):
1255				m.wordRight()
1256			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.Paste):
1257				return m, Paste
1258			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.CharacterBackward):
1259				m.characterLeft(false /* insideLine */)
1260			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.LinePrevious):
1261				m.CursorUp()
1262			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.WordBackward):
1263				m.wordLeft()
1264			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.InputBegin):
1265				m.MoveToBegin()
1266			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.InputEnd):
1267				m.MoveToEnd()
1268			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.PageUp):
1269				m.PageUp()
1270			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.PageDown):
1271				m.PageDown()
1272			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.LowercaseWordForward):
1273				m.lowercaseRight()
1274			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.UppercaseWordForward):
1275				m.uppercaseRight()
1276			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.CapitalizeWordForward):
1277				m.capitalizeRight()
1278			case key.Matches(msg, m.KeyMap.TransposeCharacterBackward):
1279				m.transposeLeft()
1280	
1281			default:
1282				m.insertRunesFromUserInput([]rune(msg.Text))
1283			}
1284	
1285		case pasteMsg:
1286			m.insertRunesFromUserInput([]rune(msg))
1287	
1288		case pasteErrMsg:
1289			m.Err = msg
1290		}
1291	
1292		// Make sure we set the content of the viewport before updating it.
1293		view := m.view()
1294		m.viewport.SetContent(view)
1295		vp, cmd := m.viewport.Update(msg)
1296		m.viewport = &vp
1297		cmds = append(cmds, cmd)
1298	
1299		if m.useVirtualCursor {
1300			m.virtualCursor, cmd = m.virtualCursor.Update(msg)
1301	
1302			// If the cursor has moved, reset the blink state. This is a small UX
1303			// nuance that makes cursor movement obvious and feel snappy.
1304			newRow, newCol := m.cursorLineNumber(), m.col
1305			if (newRow != oldRow || newCol != oldCol) && m.virtualCursor.Mode() == cursor.CursorBlink {
1306				m.virtualCursor.IsBlinked = false
1307				cmd = m.virtualCursor.Blink()
1308			}
1309			cmds = append(cmds, cmd)
1310		}
1311	
1312		m.repositionView()
1313	
1314		return m, tea.Batch(cmds...)
1315	}
1316	
1317	func (m *Model) view() string {
1318		if len(m.Value()) == 0 && m.row == 0 && m.col == 0 && m.Placeholder != "" {
1319			return m.placeholderView()
1320		}
1321		m.virtualCursor.TextStyle = m.activeStyle().computedCursorLine()
1322	
1323		var (
1324			s                strings.Builder
1325			style            lipgloss.Style
1326			newLines         int
1327			widestLineNumber int
1328			lineInfo         = m.LineInfo()
1329			styles           = m.activeStyle()
1330		)
1331	
1332		displayLine := 0
1333		for l, line := range m.value {
1334			wrappedLines := m.memoizedWrap(line, m.width)
1335	
1336			if m.row == l {
1337				style = styles.computedCursorLine()
1338			} else {
1339				style = styles.computedText()
1340			}
1341	
1342			for wl, wrappedLine := range wrappedLines {
1343				prompt := m.promptView(displayLine)
1344				prompt = styles.computedPrompt().Render(prompt)
1345				s.WriteString(style.Render(prompt))
1346				displayLine++
1347	
1348				var ln string
1349				if m.ShowLineNumbers {
1350					if wl == 0 { // normal line
1351						isCursorLine := m.row == l
1352						s.WriteString(m.lineNumberView(l+1, isCursorLine))
1353					} else { // soft wrapped line
1354						isCursorLine := m.row == l
1355						s.WriteString(m.lineNumberView(-1, isCursorLine))
1356					}
1357				}
1358	
1359				// Note the widest line number for padding purposes later.
1360				lnw := uniseg.StringWidth(ln)
1361				if lnw > widestLineNumber {
1362					widestLineNumber = lnw
1363				}
1364	
1365				strwidth := uniseg.StringWidth(string(wrappedLine))
1366				padding := m.width - strwidth
1367				// If the trailing space causes the line to be wider than the
1368				// width, we should not draw it to the screen since it will result
1369				// in an extra space at the end of the line which can look off when
1370				// the cursor line is showing.
1371				if strwidth > m.width {
1372					// The character causing the line to be wider than the width is
1373					// guaranteed to be a space since any other character would
1374					// have been wrapped.
1375					wrappedLine = []rune(strings.TrimSuffix(string(wrappedLine), " "))
1376					padding -= m.width - strwidth
1377				}
1378				if m.row == l && lineInfo.RowOffset == wl {
1379					s.WriteString(style.Render(string(wrappedLine[:lineInfo.ColumnOffset])))
1380					if m.col >= len(line) && lineInfo.CharOffset >= m.width {
1381						m.virtualCursor.SetChar(" ")
1382						s.WriteString(m.virtualCursor.View())
1383					} else {
1384						m.virtualCursor.SetChar(string(wrappedLine[lineInfo.ColumnOffset]))
1385						s.WriteString(style.Render(m.virtualCursor.View()))
1386						s.WriteString(style.Render(string(wrappedLine[lineInfo.ColumnOffset+1:])))
1387					}
1388				} else {
1389					s.WriteString(style.Render(string(wrappedLine)))
1390				}
1391				s.WriteString(style.Render(strings.Repeat(" ", max(0, padding))))
1392				s.WriteRune('\n')
1393				newLines++
1394			}
1395		}
1396	
1397		// Always show at least `m.Height` lines at all times.
1398		// To do this we can simply pad out a few extra new lines in the view.
1399		for range m.height {
1400			s.WriteString(m.promptView(displayLine))
1401			displayLine++
1402	
1403			// Write end of buffer content
1404			leftGutter := string(m.EndOfBufferCharacter)
1405			rightGapWidth := m.Width() - uniseg.StringWidth(leftGutter) + widestLineNumber
1406			rightGap := strings.Repeat(" ", max(0, rightGapWidth))
1407			s.WriteString(styles.computedEndOfBuffer().Render(leftGutter + rightGap))
1408			s.WriteRune('\n')
1409		}
1410	
1411		return s.String()
1412	}
1413	
1414	// View renders the text area in its current state.
1415	func (m Model) View() string {
1416		// XXX: This is a workaround for the case where the viewport hasn't
1417		// been initialized yet like during the initial render. In that case,
1418		// we need to render the view again because Update hasn't been called
1419		// yet to set the content of the viewport.
1420		m.viewport.SetContent(m.view())
1421		view := m.viewport.View()
1422		styles := m.activeStyle()
1423		return styles.Base.Render(view)
1424	}
1425	
1426	// promptView renders a single line of the prompt.
1427	func (m Model) promptView(displayLine int) (prompt string) {
1428		prompt = m.Prompt
1429		if m.promptFunc == nil {
1430			return prompt
1431		}
1432		prompt = m.promptFunc(PromptInfo{
1433			LineNumber: displayLine,
1434			Focused:    m.focus,
1435		})
1436		width := lipgloss.Width(prompt)
1437		if width < m.promptWidth {
1438			prompt = fmt.Sprintf("%*s%s", m.promptWidth-width, "", prompt)
1439		}
1440	
1441		return m.activeStyle().computedPrompt().Render(prompt)
1442	}
1443	
1444	// lineNumberView renders the line number.
1445	//
1446	// If the argument is less than 0, a space styled as a line number is returned
1447	// instead. Such cases are used for soft-wrapped lines.
1448	//
1449	// The second argument indicates whether this line number is for a 'cursorline'
1450	// line number.
1451	func (m Model) lineNumberView(n int, isCursorLine bool) (str string) {
1452		if !m.ShowLineNumbers {
1453			return ""
1454		}
1455	
1456		if n <= 0 {
1457			str = " "
1458		} else {
1459			str = strconv.Itoa(n)
1460		}
1461	
1462		// XXX: is textStyle really necessary here?
1463		textStyle := m.activeStyle().computedText()
1464		lineNumberStyle := m.activeStyle().computedLineNumber()
1465		if isCursorLine {
1466			textStyle = m.activeStyle().computedCursorLine()
1467			lineNumberStyle = m.activeStyle().computedCursorLineNumber()
1468		}
1469	
1470		// Format line number dynamically based on the maximum number of lines.
1471		digits := len(strconv.Itoa(m.MaxHeight))
1472		str = fmt.Sprintf(" %*v ", digits, str)
1473	
1474		return textStyle.Render(lineNumberStyle.Render(str))
1475	}
1476	
1477	// placeholderView returns the prompt and placeholder, if any.
1478	func (m Model) placeholderView() string {
1479		var (
1480			s      strings.Builder
1481			p      = m.Placeholder
1482			styles = m.activeStyle()
1483		)
1484		// word wrap lines
1485		pwordwrap := ansi.Wordwrap(p, m.width, "")
1486		// hard wrap lines (handles lines that could not be word wrapped)
1487		pwrap := ansi.Hardwrap(pwordwrap, m.width, true)
1488		// split string by new lines
1489		plines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(pwrap), "\n")
1490	
1491		for i := range m.height {
1492			isLineNumber := len(plines) > i
1493	
1494			lineStyle := styles.computedPlaceholder()
1495			if len(plines) > i {
1496				lineStyle = styles.computedCursorLine()
1497			}
1498	
1499			// render prompt
1500			prompt := m.promptView(i)
1501			prompt = styles.computedPrompt().Render(prompt)
1502			s.WriteString(lineStyle.Render(prompt))
1503	
1504			// when show line numbers enabled:
1505			// - render line number for only the cursor line
1506			// - indent other placeholder lines
1507			// this is consistent with vim with line numbers enabled
1508			if m.ShowLineNumbers {
1509				var ln int
1510	
1511				switch {
1512				case i == 0:
1513					ln = i + 1
1514					fallthrough
1515				case len(plines) > i:
1516					s.WriteString(m.lineNumberView(ln, isLineNumber))
1517				default:
1518				}
1519			}
1520	
1521			switch {
1522			// first line
1523			case i == 0:
1524				// first character of first line as cursor with character
1525				m.virtualCursor.TextStyle = styles.computedPlaceholder()
1526	
1527				ch, rest, _, _ := uniseg.FirstGraphemeClusterInString(plines[0], 0)
1528				m.virtualCursor.SetChar(ch)
1529				s.WriteString(lineStyle.Render(m.virtualCursor.View()))
1530	
1531				// the rest of the first line
1532				s.WriteString(lineStyle.Render(styles.computedPlaceholder().Render(rest)))
1533	
1534				// extend the first line with spaces to fill the width, so that
1535				// the entire line is filled when cursorline is enabled.
1536				gap := strings.Repeat(" ", max(0, m.width-lipgloss.Width(plines[0])))
1537				s.WriteString(lineStyle.Render(gap))
1538			// remaining lines
1539			case len(plines) > i:
1540				// current line placeholder text
1541				if len(plines) > i {
1542					placeholderLine := plines[i]
1543					gap := strings.Repeat(" ", max(0, m.width-uniseg.StringWidth(plines[i])))
1544					s.WriteString(lineStyle.Render(placeholderLine + gap))
1545				}
1546			default:
1547				// end of line buffer character
1548				eob := styles.computedEndOfBuffer().Render(string(m.EndOfBufferCharacter))
1549				s.WriteString(eob)
1550			}
1551	
1552			// terminate with new line
1553			s.WriteRune('\n')
1554		}
1555	
1556		m.viewport.SetContent(s.String())
1557		return styles.Base.Render(m.viewport.View())
1558	}
1559	
1560	// Blink returns the blink command for the virtual cursor.
1561	func Blink() tea.Msg {
1562		return cursor.Blink()
1563	}
1564	
1565	// Cursor returns a [tea.Cursor] for rendering a real cursor in a Bubble Tea
1566	// program. This requires that [Model.VirtualCursor] is set to false.
1567	//
1568	// Note that you will almost certainly also need to adjust the offset cursor
1569	// position per the textarea's per the textarea's position in the terminal.
1570	//
1571	// Example:
1572	//
1573	//	// In your top-level View function:
1574	//	f := tea.NewFrame(m.textarea.View())
1575	//	f.Cursor = m.textarea.Cursor()
1576	//	f.Cursor.Position.X += offsetX
1577	//	f.Cursor.Position.Y += offsetY
1578	func (m Model) Cursor() *tea.Cursor {
1579		if m.useVirtualCursor || !m.Focused() {
1580			return nil
1581		}
1582	
1583		lineInfo := m.LineInfo()
1584		w := lipgloss.Width
1585		baseStyle := m.activeStyle().Base
1586	
1587		xOffset := lineInfo.CharOffset +
1588			w(m.promptView(0)) +
1589			w(m.lineNumberView(0, false)) +
1590			baseStyle.GetMarginLeft() +
1591			baseStyle.GetPaddingLeft() +
1592			baseStyle.GetBorderLeftSize()
1593	
1594		yOffset := m.cursorLineNumber() -
1595			m.viewport.YOffset() +
1596			baseStyle.GetMarginTop() +
1597			baseStyle.GetPaddingTop() +
1598			baseStyle.GetBorderTopSize()
1599	
1600		c := tea.NewCursor(xOffset, yOffset)

Now I can see the key insight. Looking at the Bubble Tea v2 textarea, there are two key message types for handling text input:

1. `tea.PasteMsg` - sent by the Bubble Tea program when a paste event occurs (terminal-level paste)
2. `tea.KeyPressMsg` - sent for keyboard input, with `msg.Text` containing pasted text

In the `inputbox.go` `Update` method, the code catches `tea.KeyMsg` and handles it, but it only passes it to `m.textArea.Update(msg)` at the very end (line 138). However, the critical issue is:

Looking at `detailedit/controller.go`:
- At line 244, it catches `tea.KeyMsg` and handles keyboard keys
- At line 300-306, when the key is not ctrl+d, esc, ctrl+c, or confirm-discard keys, it passes the message to `c.inputBox.Update(msg)` (the inputbox)
- In inputbox.go, the Update method at line 90 catches `tea.KeyMsg` and processes autocomplete keys, then at line 138 passes to textarea

The issue is that Bubble Tea v2 sends `tea.PasteMsg` (not `tea.KeyMsg`) when a paste occurs. The `inputbox.go` Update method doesn't handle `tea.PasteMsg` - it only switches on `tea.KeyMsg`. 1. Pasted text (via bracketed paste) comes in as `tea.PasteMsg`

package detailedit

import (
	"testing"

	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/config"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data"
	dataautocomplete "github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data/autocomplete"
	popupautocomplete "github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/autocomplete"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/context"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/theme"
)

func newTestController(t *testing.T) Controller {
	t.Helper()

	cfg, err := config.ParseConfig(config.Location{
		ConfigFlag:       "../../../config/testdata/test-config.yml",
		SkipGlobalConfig: true,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	thm := theme.ParseTheme(&cfg)
	ctx := &context.ProgramContext{
		Config: &cfg,
		Theme:  thm,
		Styles: context.InitStyles(thm),
	}

	return New(ctx)
}

func testRepo() RepoRef {
	return RepoRef{
		NameWithOwner: "owner/repo",
		Owner:         "owner",
		Name:          "repo",
	}
}

func suggestions(values ...string) []popupautocomplete.Suggestion {
	items := make([]popupautocomplete.Suggestion, 0, len(values))
	for _, value := range values {
		items = append(items, popupautocomplete.Suggestion{Value: value})
	}
	return items
}

func TestEnterCommentModeResetsAutocompleteState(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c.ac.SetSuggestions(suggestions("bug", "feature"))
	c.ac.Show("bug", nil)
	require.True(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())

	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})

	require.False(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())
	require.False(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestEnterAssignModeResetsAutocompleteState(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)

	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{

Now I have a clear understanding of the bug. Let me write a test to reproduce it and then fix it.

The issue:
1. In `controller.go`'s `Update` method, `tea.PasteMsg` is not handled and doesn't reach the inputBox.
2. The controller catches `tea.KeyMsg` specifically (line 244) and passes other key messages to `c.inputBox.Update(msg)`.
3. But `tea.PasteMsg` is NOT a `tea.KeyMsg`, so it falls through to the end where only `spinner.TickMsg` and `popupautocomplete.ClearFetchStatusMsg` are handled.
4. The textarea's `Update` method properly handles `tea.PasteMsg`, but it never receives it because the controller drops it.

The fix: In the controller's `Update` method, we need to also pass `tea.PasteMsg` to the inputBox's Update method.

func TestPasteMsgReachesInputBox(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,

		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})

	pastedText := "this should paste into gh-dash"
	c, _, _, handled := c.Update(tea.PasteMsg{Content: pastedText})

package detailedit

import (
	"testing"

	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/config"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data"
	dataautocomplete "github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data/autocomplete"
	popupautocomplete "github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/autocomplete"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/context"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/theme"
)

func newTestController(t *testing.T) Controller {
	t.Helper()

	cfg, err := config.ParseConfig(config.Location{
		ConfigFlag:       "../../../config/testdata/test-config.yml",
		SkipGlobalConfig: true,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	thm := theme.ParseTheme(&cfg)
	ctx := &context.ProgramContext{
		Config: &cfg,
		Theme:  thm,
		Styles: context.InitStyles(thm),
	}

	return New(ctx)
}

func testRepo() RepoRef {
	return RepoRef{
		NameWithOwner: "owner/repo",
		Owner:         "owner",
		Name:          "repo",
	}
}

func suggestions(values ...string) []popupautocomplete.Suggestion {
	items := make([]popupautocomplete.Suggestion, 0, len(values))
	for _, value := range values {
		items = append(items, popupautocomplete.Suggestion{Value: value})
	}
	return items
}

func TestEnterCommentModeResetsAutocompleteState(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c.ac.SetSuggestions(suggestions("bug", "feature"))
	c.ac.Show("bug", nil)
	require.True(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())

	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})

	require.False(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())
	require.False(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestEnterAssignModeResetsAutocompleteState(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c.ac.SetSuggestions(suggestions("bug", "feature"))
	c.ac.Show("bug", nil)
	require.True(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())

	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeAssign,
		Prompt:                           "assign",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.WhitespaceSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	require.False(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())
	require.False(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestEnterLabelModePrepopulatesCurrentLabels(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearLabelCache()
	c := newTestController(t)

	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeLabel,
		Prompt:                           "label",
		InitialValue:                     "bug, docs, ",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.LabelSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionLabels,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	require.Equal(t, "bug, docs, ", c.inputBox.Value())
}

func TestRepoUsersFetchedUpdatesControllerState(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeAssign,

		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,

The fix is in `controller.go`. When the mode is active and we receive a `tea.PasteMsg`, we should pass it to the inputBox so it reaches the textarea. Looking at the code flow:

1. `tea.KeyMsg` → caught at line 244 → specific keys handled → otherwise passed to `c.inputBox.Update(msg)` at line 305
2. `spinner.TickMsg` and `popupautocomplete.ClearFetchStatusMsg` → caught at line 322-327  
3. Everything else → returns `nil, nil, false`

The fix is to also handle `tea.PasteMsg` when the controller is active and pass it to the inputBox. I need to add handling before the `tea.KeyMsg` is caught, or after the default return. Since `tea.PasteMsg` is NOT a `tea.KeyMsg`, it won't match at line 244.

package detailedit

import (
	"testing"

	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/config"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data"
	dataautocomplete "github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/data/autocomplete"
	popupautocomplete "github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/components/autocomplete"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/context"
	"github.com/dlvhdr/gh-dash/v4/internal/tui/theme"
)

func newTestController(t *testing.T) Controller {
	t.Helper()

	cfg, err := config.ParseConfig(config.Location{
		ConfigFlag:       "../../../config/testdata/test-config.yml",
		SkipGlobalConfig: true,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	thm := theme.ParseTheme(&cfg)
	ctx := &context.ProgramContext{
		Config: &cfg,
		Theme:  thm,
		Styles: context.InitStyles(thm),
	}

	return New(ctx)
}

func testRepo() RepoRef {
	return RepoRef{
		NameWithOwner: "owner/repo",
		Owner:         "owner",
		Name:          "repo",
	}
}

func suggestions(values ...string) []popupautocomplete.Suggestion {
	items := make([]popupautocomplete.Suggestion, 0, len(values))
	for _, value := range values {
		items = append(items, popupautocomplete.Suggestion{Value: value})
	}
	return items
}

func TestEnterCommentModeResetsAutocompleteState(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c.ac.SetSuggestions(suggestions("bug", "feature"))
	c.ac.Show("bug", nil)
	require.True(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())

	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})

	require.False(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())
	require.False(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestEnterAssignModeResetsAutocompleteState(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c.ac.SetSuggestions(suggestions("bug", "feature"))
	c.ac.Show("bug", nil)
	require.True(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())

	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeAssign,
		Prompt:                           "assign",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.WhitespaceSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	require.False(t, c.ac.HasSuggestions())
	require.False(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestEnterLabelModePrepopulatesCurrentLabels(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearLabelCache()
	c := newTestController(t)

	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeLabel,
		Prompt:                           "label",
		InitialValue:                     "bug, docs, ",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.LabelSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionLabels,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	require.Equal(t, "bug, docs, ", c.inputBox.Value())
}

func TestRepoUsersFetchedUpdatesControllerState(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeAssign,
		Prompt:                           "assign",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.WhitespaceSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	c, _, _, handled := c.Update(
		RepoUsersFetchedMsg{Users: []data.User{{Login: "alice", Name: "Alice"}}},
	)
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.Len(t, c.repoUsers, 1)
	require.Equal(t, "alice", c.repoUsers[0].Login)
	require.True(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestRepoLabelsFetchedUpdatesControllerState(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeLabel,
		Prompt:                           "label",
		InitialValue:                     "bu",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.LabelSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionLabels,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	c, _, _, handled := c.Update(
		RepoLabelsFetchedMsg{Labels: []data.Label{{Name: "bug", Description: "Bug"}}},
	)
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.Len(t, c.repoLabels, 1)
	require.Equal(t, "bug", c.repoLabels[0].Name)
	require.True(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestEnterSilentFetchReturnsCommand(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)

	_, cmd := c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeAssign,
		Prompt:                           "assign",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.WhitespaceSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchSilent,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	require.NotNil(t, cmd)
}

func TestForceRefreshClearsRelevantCache(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearLabelCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeLabel,
		Prompt:                           "label",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.LabelSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionLabels,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	_, cmd, _, handled := c.Update(popupautocomplete.FetchSuggestionsRequestedMsg{Force: true})
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.NotNil(t, cmd)
}

func TestCommentModeHidesPopupWhenMentionContextDisappears(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		InitialValue:                     "@ali",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})
	c.ac.SetSuggestions(suggestions("alice"))
	c.showSuggestionsFromCurrentContext()
	require.True(t, c.ac.IsVisible())

	c.inputBox.SetValue("done")
	c.showSuggestionsFromCurrentContext()
	require.False(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestCommentModeShowsPopupForBareAtMention(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})
	c.ac.SetSuggestions(suggestions("alice"))

	c, _, _, handled := c.Update(tea.KeyPressMsg{Text: "@"})
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.True(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestAssignModeShowsPopupForEmptyContext(t *testing.T) {
	data.ClearUserCache()
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeAssign,
		Prompt:                           "assign",
		InitialValue:                     "",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.WhitespaceSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})
	c.ac.SetSuggestions(suggestions("alice"))
	c.showSuggestionsFromCurrentContext()

	require.True(t, c.ac.IsVisible())
}

func TestEscapeInCommentModeShowsDiscardPrompt(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})

	c, _, _, handled := c.Update(tea.KeyPressMsg{Text: "esc"})
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.True(t, c.showConfirmCancel)
}

func TestConfirmDiscardExitsMode(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeApprove,
		Prompt:                           "approve",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.WhitespaceSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	c.showConfirmCancel = true
	c, _, _, handled := c.Update(tea.KeyPressMsg{Text: "y"})
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.Equal(t, ModeNone, c.Mode())
}

func TestRejectDiscardRestoresPrompt(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})

	c.showConfirmCancel = true
	c, _, _, handled := c.Update(tea.KeyPressMsg{Text: "n"})
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.False(t, c.showConfirmCancel)
}

func TestCtrlDReturnsSubmit(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeAssign,
		Prompt:                           "assign",
		InitialValue:                     "alice bob",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.WhitespaceSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: false,
	})

	c, _, submit, handled := c.Update(tea.KeyPressMsg{Text: "ctrl+d"})
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.Equal(t, ModeNone, c.Mode())
	require.NotNil(t, submit)
	require.Equal(t, ModeAssign, submit.Mode)
	require.Equal(t, "alice bob", submit.Value)
}

func TestUnassignModeDoesNotUseAutocomplete(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:         ModeUnassign,
		Prompt:       "unassign",
		InitialValue: "alice\nbob",
		Repo:         testRepo(),
	})

	require.False(t, c.usesAutocomplete())
}

func TestPasteMsgReachesInputBox(t *testing.T) {
	c := newTestController(t)
	c, _ = c.Enter(EnterOptions{
		Mode:                             ModeComment,
		Prompt:                           "comment",
		Source:                           dataautocomplete.UserMentionSource{},
		Repo:                             testRepo(),
		SuggestionKind:                   SuggestionUsers,
		EnterFetch:                       FetchNone,
		ConfirmDiscardOnCancel:           true,
		HideAutocompleteWhenContextEmpty: true,
	})

	pastedText := "this should paste into gh-dash"
	c, _, _, handled := c.Update(tea.PasteMsg{Content: pastedText})
	require.True(t, handled)
	require.Equal(t, pastedText, c.inputBox.Value())
}

content.text:
The bug has been fixed. Here's a summary:

**Root Cause:** In Bubble Tea v2, paste events come as `tea.PasteMsg` (not `tea.KeyMsg`). The `detailedit.Controller.Update()` method in `controller.go` had a `switch` statement that only handled `tea.KeyMsg` for passing keyboard input to the underlying `inputBox` (which in turn passes it to the `textarea`). `tea.PasteMsg` was not handled anywhere in the controller, so it fell through the switch and was silently dropped — the textarea never received the paste event.

**Fix:** Added a `case tea.PasteMsg:` handler in `controller.go` (at line 244) that checks if the controller is active, and if so, passes the paste message to `c.inputBox.Update(msg)`, which relays it to the Bubble Tea v2 textarea that properly handles `tea.PasteMsg`. This single added block of 5 lines ensures pasted text now flows through to the input box for both `prview` and `issueview`.

The `detailedit.Controller.Update()` method in `controller.go` had a `switch` statement that only handled `tea.KeyMsg` for passing keyboard input to the underlying `inputBox` (which in turn passes it to the `textarea`). `tea.PasteMsg` was not handled anywhere in the controller, so it fell through the switch and was silently dropped — the textarea never received the paste event.

**Fix:** Added a `case tea.PasteMsg:` handler in `controller.go` (at line 244) that checks if the controller is active, and if so, passes the paste message to `c.inputBox.Update(msg)`, which relays it to the Bubble Tea v2 textarea that properly handles `tea.PasteMsg`. This single added block of 5 lines ensures pasted text now flows through to the input box for both `prview` and `issueview`.
